content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 25804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • • Administration • • • Executive/Legal • • • Enforcement • • • Conflict of Interest
(916) 322-5662         322-5660              322-5901              322-6441              322-6444

February 14, 1980

A-80-02-078

Ms. Linda Dash
3057 Chablis Lane
Atwater, CA 95301

Dear Ms. Dash:

Thank you for your letter dated January 25, 1980, seeking informal advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act. You have asked if it is a conflict of interest for you to hold a public office with the same city that employs your spouse.

Government Code Section 87100 provides:

No public official at any level of state or local government shall make, participate in making or in any way attempt to use his official position to influence a governmental decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a financial interest.

The Political Reform Act states that a public official has a financial interest if it is foreseeable that the governmental decision will materially affect the official's interests in real property, sources of income or business entities in which he or she has an investment or holds a position of management or employment. See Government Code Section 87103.

The Political Reform Act does not prohibit you from holding office because your spouse is employed by the same government entity. Once elected, however, the Act, as previously cited above, would require that you refrain from participating in those government decisions in which you may have a financial interest.

Ms. Linda Dash
February 14, 1980

Page Two

Please note that I am able to advise you only concerning
the Political Reform Act. You may want to consult a private
attorney to find out if there are other laws that might prohibit you from holding office or restrict your actions in
office. I hope that this advice is helpful. If you have any
questions feel free to contact me.

Sincerely,

Dwight H. Dickerson
Counsel
Legal Division

DED:nrw

JAN 25 1980 0:50 AM JJ

Linda A. Dash
3057 Chablis Lane
Atwater, California 95301

January 25, 1980

Mr. Dwight Dickerson
Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Dear Mr. Dickerson:

I have taken out papers for the position of Atwater City Council.
The election will be held in April 1980.

My question pertains to my holding public office with the City
of Atwater, while my husband is employed with the City of Atwater.
Could this be a conflict of interest?

Any information you could provide would be greatly appreciated.

Yours, very truly,

Linda A. Dash
